Week One —
My Notes

Personal feedback notes from the first session exploring the InnerSeed Garden app. Week 1, Page 1. Part of a 10-week collaborative development study.

Note — Week 1 is still in progress. These notes will be added to during tomorrow’s focus call with Jeroen. This document is not yet concluded and further observations will be added before Monday 8 June, giving us a much stronger starting point for Week 2.
A note on how this document started: These notes were originally written in Google Docs — six pages worth. They didn’t survive. This version is the rebuild, and it lives somewhere safer. Week 1 covers everything from 4 June 2026 onwards. Anything from Monday 8 June will be logged as Week 2.
01

Session Builder — first look

I went straight into the Session Builder with the idea that we’d spend most of the focus call on this tomorrow. First impression: it looks clean and beautifully designed.

Fig. 1 — Session Builder, starting point, 4 June 2026

Observation

The overall design is clean and well put together. No complaints here — it’s a strong starting point visually.

UX Feedback

It would be good to make the Session Settings modal a little easier and more intuitive to find. I mentioned this again later when I was listening to a preset and wanted to return to it — both times it wasn’t obvious where to go.

The modal itself is great, but it’s currently only accessible from the small settings cog in the transport bar. The three options inside it — Preset, Builder, Creator — are essentially the core of the whole app, and they deserve to be more visible.

Fig. 2 — The Session Settings modal, currently only accessible via the settings cog

UX Feedback — two solutions

Two options worth considering for making the modal more accessible:

Option A — First launch screen

Appears once on first open. User picks their mode before anything else.

Welcome to

InnerSeed Garden

How would you like to use the app?

Preset

Ready-made sessions

Builder

Build your session

Recommended

Creator

Add your own voice

Facilitator

You can change this any time in settings

Option B — Persistent header indicator

Always visible at the top. One tap opens the mode switcher.

InnerSeed ☰ Builder mode ⌄

— app content below —

The pill in the header always shows the active mode. Tapping it opens the switcher. Never hidden, never buried in a settings cog.

Fig. 3 — Option A (first launch screen) and Option B (persistent header indicator)

UX Feedback

The three module cards — Ambient Music, Guided Breaths, Guided Voices — are the main building blocks of a session. Having all three fully visible at a glance would be better, both for usability and from an upgrade perspective. Currently Guided Voices is a bit cut off on the right.

02

Ambient Music card

Fig. 4 — Ambient Music card, expanded empty state

UX Feedback

It took me a while to realise I needed to tap the “No favorite tracks yet” box to open the Music Finder. The first word being “No” didn’t help — it reads more like an error message than an invitation to do something. Could be more intuitive.

03

Preset playback — transport & controls

Fig. 5 — Preset page, “Ethereal Voyager” playing

Fig. 6 — Transport controls, close-up view

UX Feedback

It would be good to have a bit more control over playback — the ability to pause, rewind, or jump to a different part of the session. Something like a draggable progress bar or a back-30-seconds button would help.

That said, there’s a balance here. Giving people too easy an option to stop might not actually serve them — most people don’t want to stop a breathwork session, they just think they do in the moment. So the controls should be there, but they don’t need to shout about it.

Feature Idea

What if tapping pause triggered a short kindness message before actually pausing? Something that gently encourages the person to stay with the session. The option to pause would still be there, but it would give people a moment to reconsider.

The message could change depending on where you are in the session. Near the end, something like “Only 3 minutes left — you’ve come this far” would be pretty compelling.

🌿

You set this time aside

Your breath is doing the work.
Stay with it — just one more inhale.

The pause option is still there — just not the loudest thing in the room

Fig. 7 — Pause intercept concept mockup

04

Post-session navigation — finding your way back

Once a session finishes, the path forward isn’t as clear as it could be. Here’s the current journey and where it loses momentum.

Fig. 8 — Session complete — “Go To The Green House” appears below the transport bar

Fig. 9 — The Green House — progress stats, after clicking through from the session end screen

UX Feedback

When a session finishes, a “Go To The Green House” button appears below the player. That part is a nice touch — it gives you somewhere to go. But once you’re in The Green House looking at your stats, it’s not obvious how to play another preset. There’s no clear “back to sessions” or “browse presets” route visible.

After a good breathwork session, you’re not always in the sharpest navigational mindset. The last thing you want to do is hunt around for the settings cog or figure out which sidebar icon takes you back to the session library. The path forward should be obvious and warm, not a puzzle.

The current journey is: session ends → Green House → stuck. What it should feel like is: session ends → gentle landing page → clear next step.

Feature Idea — post-session landing screen

Rather than going straight to The Green House, what if the session completion triggered a brief landing screen first? A moment to land, acknowledge the session, and offer two or three clear next steps. The Green House is still accessible, but it’s not the only option presented.

Suggestion — post-session screen

Appears immediately after a session completes, before going anywhere else.

Session complete

Ethereal Voyager

21 min · Jeroen · 1 day streak 🔥

Play another preset

Browse the full session library

Play again

Repeat Ethereal Voyager

Go to The Green House

View your progress & stats

The two primary actions are clear and inviting. The Green House link is still present but secondary — most users after a session want to either repeat or explore, not immediately check stats.

Fig. 10 — Suggested post-session landing screen concept

05

The empty space below the player

Feature Idea

There’s a large empty space below the transport controls, particularly on the Preset page. It doesn’t necessarily need to be filled, but it could be used well. My idea was to add a breathing visualiser here — something slow and ambient that moves with the breath cycle. Not distracting, just a living part of the page.

It could be a Preset-only feature, since having it in the Builder or Creator views would be more of a headache than a help. It could also be something unlocked on upgrade — the free version gets the static wave, the premium version gets the animated one.

inhale · static preview

Fig. 11 — Breathing visualiser concept, static preview

Week 1 — still in progress

These notes cover the first exploration on 4 June 2026. Week 1 is not yet concluded. A focus call with Jeroen is taking place tomorrow, and further observations will be added before Monday 8 June.

The goal is to have this document as complete as possible before Week 2 begins, so it serves as a solid foundation for everything that follows.
